style_bible.visual_style: editorial paper collage on real newsprint scan; desaturated archival plates; flat annotation plane; serif labels with hairline underlines; one hand-drawn annotation per graphic max
style_bible.color_palette: cream ground, charcoal serif type, two accents max, terracotta/yellow as the single editor's-pen voice

metadata.title: The Ultraviolet Catastrophe — Vox edition
metadata.purpose: CONVERTED from bears-doodles; editorial pass applied — 4 rewrites, 11 keeps.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
INTRO: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
OUTRO: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. This is the story of the graph that broke physics.
beats.tts_normalized_text: This is the story of the graph that broke physics.
beats.source_visual: series character draws in, then the title in Shadows Into Light
beats.new_visual_element: [CARD] title — The Ultraviolet Catastrophe / the graph that broke physics

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [CARD] title — The Ultraviolet Catastrophe / the graph that broke physics

beats.narration_text: Everything warm is quietly glowing — your body, a campfire, the sun — all giving off light.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Everything warm is quietly glowing — your body, a campfire, the sun — all giving off light.
beats.source_visual: a person warming by a campfire
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] glowing iron in a foundry / campfire, archival, slow push-in

build.needs: YOU → drop a historical image at media/H01.png (or .mp4)
build.suggested: find: [PHOTO] glowing iron in a foundry / campfire, archival, slow push-in

beats.narration_text: And at the start of the twentieth century, our best theory of light made an insane prediction: that glow should blast you with infinite ultraviolet. It doesn't.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And at the start of the twentieth century, our best theory of light made an insane prediction: that glow should blast you with infinite ultraviolet. It doesn't.
beats.source_visual: a person bracing as ultraviolet ray-lines blast toward them
beats.new_visual_element: [COMPOSITE] crimson runaway ink line drawn OVER the H01 plate on the annotation plane

annotation.note: runaway curve sweeps up and off-frame on 'infinite'

build.needs: YOU → drop a historical image at media/H02.png (or .mp4)
build.suggested: find: [COMPOSITE] crimson runaway ink line drawn OVER the H01 plate on the annotation plane

beats.narration_text: Plot how brightly an object glows against the frequency of the light it emits.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Plot how brightly an object glows against the frequency of the light it emits.
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: axes: x = frequency, y = brightness

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: axes: x = frequency, y = brightness

beats.narration_text: At low frequencies, only a few light-wave patterns fit inside the object.
beats.tts_normalized_text: At low frequencies, only a few light-wave patterns fit inside the object.
beats.source_visual: 2-3 small standing-wave arcs near the low-frequency end
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: 2-3 small standing-wave arcs near the low-frequency end

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: 2-3 small standing-wave arcs near the low-frequency end

beats.narration_text: But the number of possible patterns climbs faster and faster as the frequency rises.
beats.tts_normalized_text: But the number of possible patterns climbs faster and faster as the frequency rises.
beats.source_visual: many more arcs packing in toward high frequency; a faint dashed count curve rising as f-squared
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: many more arcs packing in toward high frequency; a faint das

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: many more arcs packing in toward high frequency; a faint das

beats.narration_text: Classical physics hands every single pattern the exact same share of energy.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Classical physics hands every single pattern the exact same share of energy.
beats.source_visual: equal-height energy bars dropping onto every arc
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: equal-height energy bars dropping onto every arc

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: equal-height energy bars dropping onto every arc

beats.narration_text: Countless patterns, each fully funded, drive the predicted brightness screaming toward infinity.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Countless patterns, each fully funded, drive the predicted brightness screaming toward infinity.
beats.source_visual: the Rayleigh-Jeans curve climbing steeply off the top of the frame (forbidden red)
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: the Rayleigh-Jeans curve climbing steeply off the top of the

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: the Rayleigh-Jeans curve climbing steeply off the top of the

beats.narration_text: But real objects stay calm — their glow rises, peaks, and gently falls.
beats.tts_normalized_text: But real objects stay calm — their glow rises, peaks, and gently falls.
beats.source_visual: fresh axes with the faint red runaway as a ghost, plus the measured curve in Warm Slate that rises, peaks, falls

beats.narration_text: In 1900, Max Planck proposed a desperate fix: energy comes only in whole chunks — never slivers.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In 1900, Max Planck proposed a desperate fix: energy comes only in whole chunks — never slivers.
beats.source_visual: a smooth ramp crossed out beside a staircase of discrete equal chunks
beats.new_visual_element: [DOCUMENT] Planck's 1900/1901 paper, zoom to the quantum hypothesis, crimson chip

document.source_doc: M. Planck, 'Ueber das Gesetz der Energieverteilung im Normalspectrum', Annalen der Physik (1901)

build.needs: YOU → drop a historical image at media/A08.png (or .mp4)
build.suggested: find: [DOCUMENT] Planck's 1900/1901 paper, zoom to the quantum hypothesis, crimson chip

beats.narration_text: Each chunk costs more at higher frequency — ultraviolet light is wildly expensive.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Each chunk costs more at higher frequency — ultraviolet light is wildly expensive.
beats.source_visual: chunks redrawn growing taller with frequency; a price tag on the tallest (UV) chunk

beats.narration_text: The warm object simply cannot afford those costly high-frequency patterns.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The warm object simply cannot afford those costly high-frequency patterns.
beats.source_visual: the spectrum's high-frequency wave arcs greying out / going empty
beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: the spectrum's high-frequency wave arcs greying out / going

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [GRAPHIC] TODO — was: the spectrum's high-frequency wave arcs greying out / going

beats.narration_text: So the expensive patterns stay empty, and the runaway curve bends back to zero.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So the expensive patterns stay empty, and the runaway curve bends back to zero.
beats.source_visual: the red runaway curve bending down to meet the Warm Slate Planck curve

beats.narration_text: Planck thought the chunks were a mathematical trick. They were the first quanta — and the fuse of everything quantum.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Planck thought the chunks were a mathematical trick. They were the first quanta — and the fuse of everything quantum.
